title: Recent Advances in Silent Gene Cluster Activation in Streptomyces
research_focus: Strategies for activating silent BGCs in Streptomyces

streptomyces_significance.antibiotic_production: About two-thirds of natural antibiotics produced by Streptomyces
streptomyces_significance.silent_bgc_percentage: ~90% of BGCs silent or cryptic under standard laboratory conditions

silent_bgc_challenge.definition: BGCs inactive or minimally expressed under standard laboratory culture conditions
silent_bgc_challenge.scope: Majority of Streptomyces secondary metabolite production potential remains untapped
silent_bgc_challenge.impact: Represents vast untapped reservoir of natural products for drug discovery

activation_strategies.approach: Clone and reconstruct BGCs in alternative host organisms
Induce production in original Streptomyces organism

chassis_engineering.definition: Modification of heterologous host to improve metabolite production

native_host_strategies.promoter_engineering: Replacement of weak native promoters with strong ones
native_host_strategies.regulatory_factors: Overexpression or silencing of regulatory proteins
native_host_strategies.environmental_induction: Chemical or physical stimuli to trigger expression

research_drivers.productivity_limitation: Discovery methods access <10% of bacterial small molecule repertoire
research_drivers.antibiotic_crisis: Rising multidrug-resistant bacteria demand new antibiotics
research_drivers.untapped_potential: Sequenced genomes reveal vast potential for natural product discovery
